description: This course provides an advanced study of theoretical and practical bases of leadership in healthcare, including concepts regarding patient advocacy and lateral integration of care. Decision-making and change processes within the context of the healthcare environment are examined. Three credit hours.

title: Rsrch_Evidence-Based Practice
description: This course explores the use of current research and practice guidelines for clinical decision-making, including problem identification and outcomes measurement. Critical appraisal processes are used to determine best practices for improving patient safety, care quality, and health outcomes through integration and dissemination of new knowledge. Graduates will be prepared to provide leadership for designing, implementing and evaluating quality improvement projects and safety initiatives to guide practice and improve outcomes. Three credit hours

title: âInstruct Design & Teach Strat
description: This course will explore educational theories, principles of teaching and learning, instructional methods, and delivery modalities in academic or clinical settings. This course will also provide instruction for assessment, measurement, and evaluation, including test construction and item analysis. The use of technology and distance learning will be examined. Three credit hours.

title: Advanced Clinical Assesment
description: This course expounds knowledge of nursing theory and practice associated with various modalities for clinical assessment of individuals, families, communities, and groups. The course enhances knowledge and clinical experience in advanced assessment of diverse clients across the life span within the context of the Clinical Nurse Leader role. The utilization of information systems technology for exploring health care outcomes, application of the nursing process through various technologies, therapeutic communication with diverse clients/groups, and patient education are explored. Prerequisite: Admission to the MSN program. Three credit hours

title: Population Health & Epidemiology
description: This course examines the determinants of health, epidemiological methods, disease risk and distribution, health disparities, and prevention strategies across populations. Students apply analytic approaches to assess needs, evaluate community-level outcomes, and develop evidence-based strategies aimed at improving population health. Prerequisite: Admission to the MSN program. Three credit hours.

title: Evidence-Based Practice & Research Translation
description: This course prepares graduate nursing students to critically appraise research and clinical evidence and translate findings into practice across diverse settings. Emphasis is placed on literature appraisal, synthesis of evidence, clinical practice guidelines, quality improvement processes, database search strategies, and evaluation of outcomes. Students develop skills to apply evidence-based decision-making to improve patient care, educational practices, and healthcare systems. Prerequisite: NUR 610 . Two credit hours.

title: App Nur Ed, Ldrshp&Pract Impro
description: NUR 699 .APPLICATION OF NURSING EDUCATION, LEADERSHIP, AND PRACTICE IMPROVEMENT This course is a culmination of didactic and practicum courses where students develop and present a concentration thesis project. One credit hour.

title: Healthcare Structure & Finance
description: This course provides an overview of the structure of U.S. healthcare systems and organizations, including legal and regulatory issues that impact the healthcare industry. The course also explores the principles of healthcare finance and economics, including reimbursement structures, resource allocation, and socioeconomic considerations. Additional topics include the role of informatics and technology utilization in healthcare. Three credit hours.

title: Foundations of Nursing Education
description: This course introduces the role of the nurse educator and the academic environment of nursing education. Emphasis is placed on educator responsibilities, distinctions between academic and clinical roles, and expectations within higher education. Content includes legal and ethical responsibilities, disability services and inclusive educational practices, academic policies, student support processes, and the regulatory and accreditation environment. The course provides a foundation for subsequent coursework in nursing education. Prerequisites: Admission to the MSN Nurse Educator Program. Three credit hours.

title: CNL Immersion Practicum III
description: Application of nursing theory and practice within the context of the Clinical Nurse Leader role is demonstrated through a major synthesis project. The project incorporates analysis of selected patient and healthcare system issues and the development of action plans for patient care and organizational change. Prerequisites: "B" or better in NUR 635 , NUR 651 and NUR 660 . Four credit hours.

title: Prin/Pract Nursing Ed
description: NUR 800 .PRINCIPLES AND PRACTICE OF NURSING EDUCATION This course is designed to provide expert nurses with a theoretical foundation to promote excellence in teaching in both faculty and professional development roles. Principles of adult learning theory, learning styles, and preferences are explored with a focus on impact of educational design. Students will review various learning domains and approaches for curriculum development including writing measurable learning objectives. Content also includes an examination of numerous didactic teaching strategies, general tactics for the use of simulation, approaches for interprofessional collaboration, and methods for evaluating learner competence. Three credit hours.

title: Instructional Strategies in Academic and Clinical Nursing Education
description: This course examines learning theories and evidence-based instructional strategies used in academic, clinical, and simulation settings. Emphasis is placed on instructional planning, selection and implementation of teaching methods, facilitation of learning activities, and adaptation of strategies to learner level and educational context. Course content addresses teaching in classroom, clinical, simulation, and online environments. Prerequisites: B or higher in NURE 620 . Three credit hours.

title: Practicum I: Clinical and Simulation Teaching
description: This course provides supervised experiential learning focused on clinical and simulation teaching roles within nursing education. Students engage in instructional activities such as skills instruction, simulation facilitation, clinical supervision, and learner feedback. Practicum experiences include supervised engagement in advanced-level direct patient care activities that support clinical teaching, skill demonstration, and the development of clinical judgment. Emphasis is placed on integration of teaching strategies, professional communication, and reflective practice within educational settings. Corequisites or Prerequisites: NURE 641 and NURE 640 ; B or higher if a prerequisite. One credit hour.

title: Practicum II: Academic Nursing Education
description: This course provides supervised experiential learning in academic nursing education settings. Students participate in classroom instruction, course support activities, assessment processes, and student engagement under faculty supervision. Emphasis is placed on the application of instructional strategies, assessment methods, and professional educator responsibilities. Prerequisite: B or higher in NURE 640 and NURE 641 . Two credit hours.

This course is a culminating seminar for the Nurse Educator track focused on synthesis, professional role transition, and readiness for educator practice. Through structured seminar discussions, guided reflection, and applied scholarly work, students integrate knowledge and experiences from prior coursework. Course requirements include completion of a capstone project addressing an educational issue in nursing education and development of a professional teaching portfolio. Emphasis is placed on reflective practice, professional identity formation, and preparation for nurse educator roles. Corequisite: NURE 691 . Prerequisite B or higher in NURE 640 and NURE 641 . Two credit hours.

title: Organizational Behavior and Change Management
description: This course explores human behavior, organizational culture, and systems thinking within healthcare organizations. It examines the role of nurse leaders in managing change, leading teams, and navigating organizational dynamics. Emphasis is placed on strategies to improve performance, support effective change initiatives, and foster a positive and professional work environment. Prerequisite: Admission to the MSN program. Three credit hours.

title: Healthcare Information Systems and Data Utilization
description: This course examines informatics, artificial intelligence, and digital technologies used in healthcare management. It focuses on how nurse managers use data analytics, electronic health records, and clinical decision support systems to support operational decision-making, monitor performance, and improve patient outcomes. Prerequisite: Admission to the MSN program. Three credit hours.

title: HR and Workforce Development
description: This course focuses on strategic human resource management within healthcare organizations. Students explore advanced concepts in recruitment, retention, performance evaluation, and workforce planning. Emphasis is placed on applying leadership strategies that foster engagement, inclusivity, and professional development to build a resilient and high-performing nursing workforce. Prerequisite: Admission to the MSN program. Two credit hours.

title: Leadership and Management Assessment Practicum
description: This practicum provides supervised, experiential learning focused on the assessment of leadership and management practices within healthcare settings. Students analyze organizational structures, workflows, and managerial processes, and develop a leadership or management-focused improvement project proposal. Prerequisite: “B” or better in NUR 613 , NUR 616 , and NURL 620 . Three credit hours.

title: Finance for Nurse Leaders
description: This course covers financial and budgeting principles relevant to healthcare organizations, with a focus on the role of nurse leaders in financial management. It examines budgeting processes, financial statements, reimbursement models, and cost-control strategies that influence organizational performance. Emphasis is placed on fiscal responsibility, effective resource allocation, and cost-benefit analysis to support sound management decisions, strategic planning, and sustainable healthcare operations. Prerequisite: Admission to the MSN program. Three credit hours.

title: Technology, Simulation and Artificial Intelligence in Nursing Education
description: This course explores the use of educational technologies in nursing education, including learning management systems, simulation, and emerging digital tools. Emphasis is placed on instructional design, ethical use of technology, learner engagement, and evaluation of technology-enhanced teaching strategies in academic and clinical settings. Course content includes critical examination of the impact of artificial intelligence on teaching and learning in nursing education, including academic integrity, equity, data privacy, faculty workload, and unintended consequences for student learning. Prerequisites: B or higher in NURE 620 . Three credit hours.

title: Curriculum Design and Program Evaluation
description: This course focuses on curriculum development and evaluation processes in nursing education. Emphasis is placed on course and program design, alignment of outcomes, teaching strategies, and assessment methods. Course content includes curriculum frameworks, program outcomes, use of evaluation data, and continuous quality improvement processes to support educational effectiveness and accreditation expectations. Prerequisite: B or higher in NURE 618 . Three credit hours.

title: Applied Leadership and Management Seminar
description: This seminar course centers on the dissemination and evaluation of applied leadership and management projects. Students present findings, participate in structured peer feedback, and examine the implications of their work for healthcare operations and leadership practice. Prerequisite: NURL 691 . One credit hour.

title: Psyc for Clinical Populations
description: This course investigates the behavior of patients in a clinical setting. Students will learn to use observation and evaluation tools to assess a patient's understanding of their disease and/or disability. Various education and behavior change techniques to promote adherence to healthy behaviors through a patient-centered approach will be explored. Three credit hours.

title: Teaching Pro Nurses Pract Envi
description: NUR 820 .TEACHING PROFESSIONAL NURSES IN THE PRACTICE ENVIRONMENT This course provides students with the fundamental knowledge and skills in preparation for teaching professional nurses in a practice environment. The course will utilize the most current American Nurses Association Nursing Professional Development Scope & Standards of Practice as a framework for understanding roles and responsibilities of nursing professional development. This course will also utilize the Clinical Nurse Leader competencies as a foundation for equipping students with knowledge and skills in organizational and systems leadership, quality improvement and safety, translating and integrating scholarship into practice, use of informatics and technologies to analyze and improve care practices, and health policy and advocacy. Three credit hours.

title: Leadership and Management Implementation Practicum
description: This practicum builds on the assessment practicum and focuses on the implementation and evaluation of a leadership or management-focused improvement project. Students apply managerial strategies to address organizational challenges, monitor outcomes, and evaluate the effectiveness of leadership and management interventions in healthcare environments. Prerequisite: “B” or better in NURL 690 . Three credit hours.

title: Assessment and Evaluation of Educational Outcomes
description: This course addresses principles and methods of assessing and evaluating learner performance in nursing education. Emphasis is placed on formative and summative assessment, test construction, use of rubrics, clinical evaluation methods, and provision of feedback. Course content supports fair, consistent, and outcome-aligned evaluation of student learning. Prerequisites: B or higher in NURE 640 and NURE 641 . Two credit hours.

title: Mgmt & Leader in Clinical Exer
description: Management and Leadership in Clinical Exercise Settings. This course is designed to provide an in-depth understanding of the government and industry standards and guidelines within the clinical setting. Students will learn industry-accepted scopes of practice, ethical, legal, and business standards. Three credit hours.

title: Teach Content Area Read Writin
description: This course is a study of methods, materials, and competencies useful for integrating reading and writing instruction into subject matter instruction to meet the needs of elementary/middle level learners. The course includes current research conducted in exemplary elementary/middle schools. Practicum embedded in this course. This course meets the SC Read to Succeed requirements. Three credit hours.

title: Integ SecondaryCurr/Ins Divers
description: This course is a study of teaching methodologies utilized in special education resource and self-contained classrooms and as support in more inclusive environments for students with mild to moderate disabilities. Major emphasis is placed on ITP development, classroom management and integrating standards based curriculum in secondary settings. Prerequisites: SPED 651 . Co-requisite: EDUC 681 . Three credit hours.

title: Instructi Pract Teach Literacy
description: This course focuses on instructional approaches, structures, and materials needed to create a comprehensive, integrated, and balanced curriculum using assessments to support all students' learning in reading and writing. This course meets the SC Read to Succeed requirements. Three credit hours.

title: Math Pedagogy Diverse Learners
description: This course is an exploration of problem based teaching methods associated with the P-12 curriculum strands for mathematics (number and operations, algebra, geometry, measurement, and data analysis and probability). Special emphasis is placed on instructional implementation of the NCTM Process Standards (problem solving, reasoning and proof, communication, connections, and representations). Three credit hours.

title: Foundations of Reading
description: This course focuses on the development of basic reading and writing processes. Content includes phonological awareness, phonics, fluency, vocabulary, comprehension, and oral language. Emphasis is placed on the methods and techniques of teaching reading and writing to individuals and groups in classroom settings. A practicum is imbedded in this course. This course meets the SC Read to Succeed requirements. Three credit hours.

title: ECED/ELEM Curr/Ins Divers Lear
description: This course will present the teaching methodologies utilized in the special education resource and self-contained classroom and as support in more inclusive environments for students with mild to moderate disabilities. Major emphasis will be placed on IEP development, classroom management, and integrating standards-based curriculum in early childhood and elementary classrooms. Prerequisites: SPED 651 . Co-requisite: EDUC 680 . Three credit hours.

title: Characteristi Diverse Learners
description: This course is a study of the characteristics, nature, and needs that distinguish individuals with mild to moderate disabilities from the general population. Focus is on definitions, associated learner characteristics, Individualized Education Programs (IEP), and instructional provisions for individuals with mental, learning, and emotional disabilities as well as autism and traumatic brain injury. Three credit hours.

title: Assess/Teaching Read to Needs
description: This course will provide students with an overview of research-based methods for assessing and teaching reading to students with and without exceptional learning needs from a variety of theoretical perspectives. Emphasis will be placed on the methods and techniques for teaching reading to individuals and groups with mild to moderate disabilities and speakers of a second language. A practicum is imbedded in this course This course meets the SC Read to Succeed requirements. Three credit hours.
